name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
description: India's flagship crop insurance scheme providing comprehensive risk coverage against crop failure due to non-preventable natural risks from pre-sowing to post-harvest.
eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas
Sharecroppers and tenant farmers with insurable interest
Both loanee and non-loanee farmers (voluntary for non-loanee since Kharif 2020)
benefits: Financial support covering prevented sowing, standing crop loss, post-harvest losses (up to 14 days), and localized calamities. Full sum insured with no capping.
premium_rate: Kharif: 2% of sum insured; Rabi: 1.5%; Commercial/Horticultural: 5%
how_to_apply: Visit nearest bank branch or CSC (Common Service Centre)
Apply online at pmfby.gov.in
Required documents: Aadhaar card, land records, bank account details, sowing certificate
Apply within 2 weeks of sowing date
claim_process: Crop Cutting Experiments (CCE) + remote sensing technology. Claims settled within 2 months via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to bank account.

name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
description: Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year to all landholding farmer families, paid in three equal installments every four months via Direct Benefit Transfer.
eligibility: All landholding farmer families with cultivable land
Small and marginal farmers prioritized
Exclusions: institutional landholders, income tax payers, government servants, professionals
benefits: ₹6,000 per year in 3 installments of ₹2,000 each (Apr-Jul, Aug-Nov, Dec-Mar)
how_to_apply: Register at pmkisan.gov.in or visit local Patwari/Revenue officer
Required: Aadhaar card, land ownership documents, bank account with IFSC
State government validates land records
e-KYC mandatory for receiving payments

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
description: Provides affordable short-term credit to farmers for crop production, post-harvest expenses, and consumption needs at subsidized interest rates (4% with prompt repayment).
eligibility: All farmers — individual/joint owners, tenant farmers, sharecroppers, SHGs
Also covers fisheries and animal husbandry sectors
Must have valid land records or tenancy agreement
benefits: Crop loan up to ₹3 lakh at 4% interest (7% minus 3% interest subvention for prompt repayment). No collateral required up to ₹1.6 lakh. Personal accident insurance of ₹50,000.
credit_limit: Based on operational land holding, cropping pattern, and scale of finance
how_to_apply: Apply at any commercial bank, cooperative bank, or RRB
Required: land records, identity proof, passport-size photo
One-page application form available at bank branches
Approval typically within 14 days

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
description: Government scheme to issue Soil Health Cards to all farmers carrying crop-wise recommendations for nutrients and fertilizers based on scientific soil testing.
eligibility: All farmers across India
Free of cost soil testing
Cards issued every 2 years per farm holding
benefits: Free soil test with recommendations for 6 nutrients (N, P, K, S, Zn, Fe). Helps optimize fertilizer use, reduce costs by 10-15%, and improve crop yield by 5-8%.
how_to_apply: Register at soilhealth.dac.gov.in
Visit nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) or soil testing lab
Collect soil sample from field (6-inch depth) and submit
Receive digital or printed Soil Health Card within 30-45 days

name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchai Yojana (PMKSY)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture / Ministry of Jal Shakti
description: Per Drop More Crop — provides subsidies for micro-irrigation (drip and sprinkler) to improve water use efficiency, reduce costs, and increase farm productivity.
eligibility: All categories of farmers
55% subsidy for small/marginal farmers; 45% for others
Higher subsidy (up to 90%) in certain NE states and hilly areas
benefits: 55% subsidy on drip/sprinkler systems. Saves 30-60% water, increases yield by 20-40%. Fertigation capability reduces fertilizer costs by 25%.
how_to_apply: Apply through state agriculture department or district horticulture office
Online application on respective state portal
Required: land documents, Aadhaar, bank account, water source proof
Select empanelled supplier for micro-irrigation system installation

name: e-NAM (National Agriculture Market)
description: Online trading platform connecting APMC mandis across India, enabling farmers to sell produce to buyers across state borders at competitive prices.
eligibility: All farmers with produce to sell
Must register at nearest e-NAM enabled mandi
Free registration
benefits: Access to buyers across 1,361 mandis in 23 states. Transparent price discovery, online payment, reduced intermediaries, better price realization (5-15% more than traditional mandi).
how_to_apply: Register at enam.gov.in or visit e-NAM enabled mandi
Required: Aadhaar, bank account, produce details
Quality testing done at mandi gate
Payment directly to bank account within 3 days

name: PM Kisan Maandhan Yojana
description: Voluntary contributory pension scheme for small and marginal farmers. Government matches farmer's monthly contribution to provide ₹3,000/month pension after age 60.
eligibility: Small and marginal farmers aged 18-40 years
Landholding up to 2 hectares
Not covered under other social security schemes
benefits: ₹3,000 per month pension after age 60. Monthly contribution ₹55-200 based on age of entry. Equal contribution by government. Family pension for spouse.
how_to_apply: Register at pmkmy.gov.in or nearest CSC
Required: Aadhaar, bank account, age proof
Monthly auto-debit from bank account
CSC operator charges ₹30 for registration

name: Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF)
description: ₹1 lakh crore financing facility for post-harvest management infrastructure — cold storage, warehouses, processing units, grading/sorting facilities.
eligibility: Farmers, FPOs, PACS, cooperatives, startups, agri-entrepreneurs
Loan up to ₹2 crore per project
Multiple projects allowed per beneficiary
benefits: 3% interest subvention on loans up to ₹2 crore. CGTMSE credit guarantee fee waiver. Moratorium period available. Loan tenure up to 10 years.
how_to_apply: Apply online at agriinfra.dac.gov.in
Prepare Detailed Project Report (DPR)
Apply through any scheduled commercial bank, NBFC, or cooperative bank
Sanction typically within 60 days

name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
description: Promotes organic farming through cluster-based approach. Provides financial assistance of ₹50,000 per hectare over 3 years for organic inputs, certification, and marketing.
eligibility: Group of 50+ farmers in a cluster covering 50+ hectares
Willing to convert to organic for minimum 3 years
Land should be in contiguous block for certification
benefits: ₹50,000/ha over 3 years (₹31,000 for organic inputs, ₹8,800 for value addition, ₹5,000 for certification, ₹5,200 for packaging/transport). PGS-India organic certification.
how_to_apply: Form a Local Group of 50 farmers with 50 hectares
Apply through district agriculture office
Register on pgsindia-ncof.gov.in for certification
Submit organic management plan for 3 years

name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization (SMAM)
description: Provides subsidies on purchase of farm machinery and equipment. Also supports Custom Hiring Centers (CHCs) for shared machinery access.
eligibility: All farmers; higher subsidy for SC/ST/small/marginal farmers
40-50% subsidy on equipment purchase
80% subsidy for establishment of CHCs by cooperatives/FPOs
benefits: 40-50% subsidy on tractors, harvesters, seed drills, sprayers, threshers, etc. Up to 80% subsidy for Custom Hiring Centers. Free training on machinery operation.
how_to_apply: Apply on agrimachinery.nic.in or state portal
Required: Aadhaar, land records, bank account, dealer quotation
Selection through lottery in case of excess applications
Subsidy credited directly to bank after purchase verification
